model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Beta project card
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=242 dy=-318 conf=1.00 -> (426,108)
  iter 1: at (426,108) said dx=-40 dy=170 conf=1.00 -> (386,278)
  iter 2: at (406,193) said dx=66 dy=8 conf=1.00 -> (472,201)
  iter 3: at (422,195) said dx=-35 dy=85 conf=1.00 -> (388,280)
  iter 4: at (414,216) said dx=60 dy=-20 conf=1.00 -> (474,196)
  iter 5: at (429,211) said dx=-43 dy=67 conf=1.00 -> (386,278)
  iter 6: at (418,228) said dx=-33 dy=52 conf=1.00 -> (385,280)
  iter 7: at (410,241) said dx=59 dy=-50 conf=1.00 -> (469,191)
  iter 8: at (424,228) said dx=-40 dy=50 conf=1.00 -> (384,278)
  iter 9: at (414,241) said dx=-29 dy=39 conf=1.00 -> (386,280)
  iter 10: at (407,251) said dx=68 dy=-65 conf=1.00 -> (475,186)
  iter 11: at (424,234) said dx=-39 dy=46 conf=1.00 -> (385,280)
  iter 12: at (414,246) said dx=-29 dy=34 conf=1.00 -> (386,280)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click on the "Beta" project card.
The current crosshair is at (407, 254) as indicated by the bottom right text (though it says (407, 254) +/- 30px, it's a good reference). Let's look at the image.
The red crosshair is inside the Beta project card.
The Beta project card is the second one in the first row.
The crosshair is currently positioned slightly below the center of the Beta card.
The center of the Beta card seems to be roughly around x=386, y=280 based on previous iterations, but looking at the image, the crosshair is at (407, 254).
Wait, the previous iterations are confusing. Let's just look at the current image.
The red crosshair is at roughly (407, 254).
The Beta card is bounded roughly by:
Left: 268
Right: 504
Top: 184
Bottom: 374
Center of Beta card: x = (268+504)/2 = 386, y = (184+374)/2 = 279.
Current crosshair: (407, 254).
dx = 386 - 407 = -21
dy = 279 - 254 = 25
Looking at the image, the crosshair is a bit to the right and a bit above the center of the card.
Let's refine the card boundaries.
Left edge of Beta card is around 268.
Right edge of Beta card is around 504.
Top edge is around 184.
Bottom edge is around 374.
Center X: (268+504)/2 = 386.
Center Y: (184+374)/2 = 279.
Crosshair is at (407, 254).
dx = 386 - 407 = -21.
dy = 279 - 254 = 25.
Let's re-estimate from the image.
The crosshair is definitely a bit to the right of the center of the 'Beta' card.
The 'Beta' text is roughly centered horizontally in the card. The crosshair is to the right of the 'B' and 'e' of Beta.
Center X seems to be around 386.
Current X is 407. dx = -21.
Center Y seems to be around 279.
Current Y is 254. dy = 25.
Wait, let's check the previous iterations.
Iter 12: at (414,246) said dx=-29 dy=34 -> (385, 280).
Current is at (407, 254).
If target is (385, 280), then dx = 385 - 407 = -22, dy = 280 - 254 = 26.
This matches my estimate.
